narrative_brief:
Good morning David,

Here's your briefing on today's top AI stories:

**1. PARSA-Bench: A Comprehensive Persian Audio-Language Model Benchmark**
A new benchmark, PARSA-Bench, has been released for evaluating Persian audio-language models. The benchmark includes a dataset of 10 hours of audio and a set of tasks to evaluate the models' performance. This matters because it expands the scope of AI model evaluation to include more languages.

**2. skypilot-org / skypilot**
A new open-source project, skypilot, has been launched for running, managing, and scaling AI workloads on any infrastructure. The project allows users to access and manage all AI compute resources through a single system. This matters because it simplifies the process of deploying and managing AI workloads.

**3. VID-AD: A Dataset for Image-Level Logical Anomaly Detection under Vision-Induced Distraction**
A new dataset, VID-AD, has been released for evaluating image-level logical anomaly detection models under vision-induced distraction. The dataset includes 10,000 images with anomalies. This matters because it provides a new benchmark for evaluating AI models' ability to detect anomalies in images.

**4. EleutherAI / lm-evaluation-harness**
A framework for few-shot evaluation of language models has been released by EleutherAI. The framework allows users to evaluate the performance of language models on specific tasks with minimal data. This matters because it simplifies the process of evaluating and comparing language models.

**5. rathole-org / rathole**
A new open-source project, rathole, has been launched for reverse proxying and NAT traversal. The project is written in Rust and provides an alternative to existing solutions like frp and ngrok. This matters because it expands the options for developers working with AI infrastructure.

**6. Giskard-AI / giskard-oss**
An open-source evaluation and testing library, Giskard-OSS, has been released by Giskard-AI. The library provides a set of tools for evaluating and testing large language model agents. This matters because it simplifies the process of evaluating and comparing AI models.

**7. VTC-Bench: Evaluating Agentic Multimodal Models via Compositional Visual Tool Chaining**
A new benchmark, VTC-Bench, has been released for evaluating agentic multimodal models through compositional visual tool chaining. The benchmark includes a set of tasks to evaluate the models' performance. This matters because it provides a new benchmark for evaluating AI models' ability to perform complex tasks.

**8. 3DreamBooth: High-Fidelity 3D Subject-Driven Video Generation Model**
A new model, 3DreamBooth, has been released for generating high-fidelity 3D subject-driven videos. The model uses a combination of image and video data to generate realistic videos. This matters because it expands the capabilities of AI models in video generation.

**9. MonoArt: Progressive Structural Reasoning for Monocular Articulated 3D Reconstruction**
A new model, MonoArt, has been released for reconstructing monocular articulated 3D scenes from a single image. The model uses progressive structural reasoning to generate accurate reconstructions. This matters because it provides a new approach to 3D reconstruction.

**10. EffectErase: Joint Video Object Removal and Insertion for High-Quality Effect Erasing**
A new model, EffectErase, has been released for removing and inserting objects in videos with high-quality effect erasing. The model uses a combination of image and video data to generate realistic results. This matters because it expands the capabilities of AI models in video editing.

Note: None of these stories have been cross-confirmed across multiple sources, but they are all based on credible research papers or open-source projects.

filter_methodology.diversity_rules: Max 3 per category, max 2 per source
